Founded in 1950 as *Dinamo Bacău*, the club spent 42 seasons in the top-flight of the [Romanian football](/wiki/Romanian_football_league_system "Romanian football league system"), winning a [Romanian League Cup](/wiki/Cupa_Ligii "Cupa Ligii") and qualifying for [European competitions](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") such as the [Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "UEFA Cup Winners' Cup") and the [Intertoto Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "UEFA Intertoto Cup")[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].
and enrolled the senior squad in the [Liga IV](/wiki/Liga_IV "Liga IV")[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Romanian football club

Not to be confused with [ACS Gauss Bacău](/wiki/ACS_Gauss_Bac%C4%83u "ACS Gauss Bacău"), [CSM Bacău (football)](/wiki/CSM_Bac%C4%83u_(football) "CSM Bacău (football)"), or [ACS FC Bacău](/wiki/ACS_FC_Bac%C4%83u "ACS FC Bacău").

Football club

FCM Bacău

**Asociația Sportivă a Suporterilor FCM 1950 Bacău** (also known as **ASS FCM 1950 Bacău**, **FCM 1950 Bacău** or **FCM Bacău**) was a Romanian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/Football_team "Football team") based in [Bacău](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u "Bacău"), [Bacău County](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u_County "Bacău County"), [Moldavia](/wiki/Moldavia "Moldavia"). Founded in 1950 as *Dinamo Bacău*, the club spent 42 seasons in the top-flight of the [Romanian football](/wiki/Romanian_football_league_system "Romanian football league system"), winning a [Romanian League Cup](/wiki/Cupa_Ligii "Cupa Ligii") and qualifying for [European competitions](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") such as the [Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "UEFA Cup Winners' Cup") and the [Intertoto Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "UEFA Intertoto Cup")[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].

In the early 2010s, the club entered in a shadowy cone due to the conflict between the team's owner, Dumitru Sechelariu (former mayor of [Bacău](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u "Bacău")) and the newly elected mayor, who chose to retire the public financing of the team and to sustain [SC Bacău](/wiki/ACS_Gauss_Bac%C4%83u "ACS Gauss Bacău")[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]. Health problems, and subsequently, the premature death of Dumitru Sechelariu in early 2013, multiplied the existing financial problems of the club. This caused FCM Bacău to withdraw from the beginning of the [2013–14 Liga III](/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_Liga_III "2013–14 Liga III") season, before dissolving later on[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].

In 2017, the Supporters Association of FCM Bacău, supported by Sergiu Sechelariu, brother of Dumitru and legal owner of the "FCM 1950 Bacău brand", started a collaboration with [Gauss Bacău](/wiki/ACS_Gauss_Bac%C4%83u "ACS Gauss Bacău") (former SC Bacău) team also left in the meantime without financing by the municipality, but the collaboration broken in the summer of 2018 and after a year of inactivity, the fans (supported again by Sechelariu's brother) started a new project named **ASS FCM 1950 Bacău** and enrolled the senior squad in the [Liga IV](/wiki/Liga_IV "Liga IV")[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Early years (1950–1992)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early years (1950–1992)")]

FCM Bacău was founded in 1950 as Dinamo Bacău under the [Ministry of Internal Affairs](/wiki/Ministry_of_Internal_Affairs_(Romania) "Ministry of Internal Affairs (Romania)") with other clubs which bore the name "Dinamo". Its management decided to separate from the ministry, renaming it Sport Club Bacău and adding [athletics](/wiki/Sport_of_athletics "Sport of athletics") and [tennis](/wiki/Tennis "Tennis").[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1)

Dinamo Bacău went undefeated in the [1952](/wiki/1952_Regional_Championship "1952 Regional Championship") season of the [Bacău Regional Championship](/wiki/Liga_IV_Bac%C4%83u "Liga IV Bacău"), finishing 1st, 11 points ahead of the 2nd-placed team, and passed Știința Câmpulung Moldovenesc, [Locomotiva Galați](/wiki/Locomotiva_Gala%C8%9Bi "Locomotiva Galați"), and [Metalul Brăila](/wiki/Metalul_Br%C4%83ila "Metalul Brăila") in successive rounds of the promotion play-offs for Divizia B. The team also reached the Round of 16 in the [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României"), eliminating Divizia B sides Locomotiva Iași and [Metalul București](/wiki/Metalul_Bucure%C8%99ti "Metalul București") before losing 2–6 to [CCA București](/wiki/CSA_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti_(football) "CSA Steaua București (football)"). The squad, coached by M. Popescu, included Anghel, Magheț, Weber, Guran, Birăescu, Olaru, Dinu, Oprea, [Coidum](/wiki/%C8%98tefan_Coidum "Ștefan Coidum"), Chiriță, Marinescu, Zimba, and Haimovici.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In Divizia B, Dinamo Bacău finished 4th in Series I during the [1953](/wiki/1953_Divizia_B "1953 Divizia B") season before moving to Series II, where they were runners-up in [1954](/wiki/1954_Divizia_B "1954 Divizia B") and won the series in [1955](/wiki/1955_Divizia_B "1955 Divizia B"), earning promotion to the top division under coach [Florian Ambru](/wiki/Florian_Ambru "Florian Ambru"). The squad comprised Varga, Miu, Weber, Chiriță, Matei, Cimaru, Ivanenko, Ioanovici, Csatary, [Oaidă](/wiki/Nicolae_Oaid%C4%83 "Nicolae Oaidă"), Sîrbu, Lemnăru, Crețea, Șoimu, Stancu, and David. In the [1956](/wiki/1956_Divizia_A "1956 Divizia A") season of [Divizia A](/wiki/Divizia_A "Divizia A"), the club finished last and was relegated to the second division.[[3]](#cite_note-L2-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Dinamo_Bac%C4%83u_%28fotbal%29_1967.jpg/330px-Dinamo_Bac%C4%83u_%28fotbal%29_1967.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dinamo_Bac%C4%83u_(fotbal)_1967.jpg)

The club played under the name *Dinamo Bacău* from 1950 until 1970

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Emerich_Dembrovschi_1970.jpg/250px-Emerich_Dembrovschi_1970.jpg)](/wiki/File:Emerich_Dembrovschi_1970.jpg)

Former Bacău player [Emerich Dembrovschi](/wiki/Emerich_Dembrovschi "Emerich Dembrovschi")

Returning to Divizia A, Dinamo Bacău finished 6th in the [1967–68](/wiki/1967%E2%80%9368_Divizia_A "1967–68 Divizia A") season and 5th in [1968–69](/wiki/1968%E2%80%9369_Divizia_A "1968–69 Divizia A") under the leadership of [Nicolae Dumitru](/wiki/Nicolae_Dumitru_(footballer) "Nicolae Dumitru (footballer)"), earning qualification for the European [Inter-Cities Fairs Cup](/wiki/Inter-Cities_Fairs_Cup "Inter-Cities Fairs Cup").[[6]](#cite_note-beginnings-6)

In the [European campaign](/wiki/1969%E2%80%9370_Inter-Cities_Fairs_Cup "1969–70 Inter-Cities Fairs Cup") Dinamo Bacău, coached by [Valeriu Neagu](/wiki/Valeriu_Neagu "Valeriu Neagu"), advanced to the Second Round, eliminating Maltese side [Floriana](/wiki/Floriana_F.C. "Floriana F.C.") (6–0 at home and 1–0 away), then overcame Norwegian [Skeid](/wiki/Skeid_Fotball "Skeid Fotball") after a 0–0 draw at [Ullevaal Stadion](/wiki/Ullevaal_Stadion "Ullevaal Stadion") and a 2–0 victory at the [23 August Stadium](/wiki/Municipal_Stadium_(Bac%C4%83u) "Municipal Stadium (Bacău)"), and defeated Scottish club [Kilmarnock](/wiki/Kilmarnock_F.C. "Kilmarnock F.C.") in the Third Round with a 1–1 draw at [Rugby Park](/wiki/Rugby_Park "Rugby Park") and a 2–0 home win. In the quarter-finals, they faced English side [Arsenal](/wiki/Arsenal_F.C. "Arsenal F.C."), losing decisively 0–2 at home and 1–7 at [Highbury Stadium](/wiki/Highbury_Stadium "Highbury Stadium"). Domestically, Dinamo ranked 9th in Divizia A in [1969–70](/wiki/1969%E2%80%9370_Divizia_A "1969–70 Divizia A") and reached the Round of 16 in [Cupa României](/wiki/1969%E2%80%9370_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1969–70 Cupa României"), losing 0–2 to [Petrolul Ploiești](/wiki/Petrolul_Ploie%C8%99ti "Petrolul Ploiești"). The squad for that season included Aristide Ghiță, Constantin Fugaciu, Vasile Panait, Alexandru Comănescu, Mircea Nedelcu, Laurențiu Velicu, Madocsa Kiss, Constantin Duțan, Nicolae Vătafu, Mircea Pană, [Emerich Dembrovschi](/wiki/Emerich_Dembrovschi "Emerich Dembrovschi"), Daniel Ene, Petre Băluță, Francisc Neumayer, and Constantin David.[[6]](#cite_note-beginnings-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Sport Club Bacău, as the club was known from 1970, continued to perform solidly in the domestic league, finishing 10th in [1970–71](/wiki/1970%E2%80%9371_Divizia_A "1970–71 Divizia A") and reaching the Round of 16 in [Cupa României](/wiki/1970%E2%80%9371_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1970–71 Cupa României"), where they lost to second-division side [Progresul Brăila](/wiki/Dacia_Unirea_Br%C4%83ila "Dacia Unirea Brăila"). The [following season](/wiki/1971%E2%80%9372_Divizia_A "1971–72 Divizia A") saw them finish 6th, again exiting [the Cup](/wiki/1971%E2%80%9372_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1971–72 Cupa României") in the Round of 16 after a 0–1 defeat to [Jiul Petroșani](/wiki/Jiul_Petro%C8%99ani "Jiul Petroșani"). In the [1972–73](/wiki/1972%E2%80%9373_Divizia_A "1972–73 Divizia A") campaign, the club achieved its best league result, 4th place, and reached the quarter-finals of [Cupa României](/wiki/1972%E2%80%9373_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1972–73 Cupa României"), losing on penalties to [Constructorul Galați](/wiki/Constructorul_Gala%C8%9Bi "Constructorul Galați"). However, the next season brought an unexpected setback, as the team was relegated at the end of [1973–74](/wiki/1973%E2%80%9374_Divizia_A "1973–74 Divizia A") after finishing 17th, while also losing in the Round of 16 of [Cupa României](/wiki/1973%E2%80%9374_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1973–74 Cupa României") 1–3 to [Steaua București](/wiki/CSA_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti_(football) "CSA Steaua București (football)"). The squad for that match comprised Voinea, Margasoiu, Catargiu, Velicu, Volmer, Hrițcu (Sinăuceanu 66'), Florea, Pană, Dembrovschi, Munteanu (Pruteanu 66'), and Băluțiu.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

*Băcăuanii* managed to bounce back the following year, winning Series I of Divizia B in the [1974–75](/wiki/1974%E2%80%9375_Divizia_B "1974–75 Divizia B") campaign under the guidance of Mircea Nedelcu and Nicolae Vătafu in the first part of the season, and [Gheorghe Constantin](/wiki/Gheorghe_Constantin "Gheorghe Constantin") in the second. The promotion-winning squad included [Coman](/wiki/Narcis_Coman "Narcis Coman"), Ursache, Pruteanu, Catargiu, Cărpuci, Lunca, Margasoiu, Sinăuceanu, Botez, Sosu, Florea, Hrițcu, Pană, Volmer, Popa, Duțan, Băluță, [Chitaru](/wiki/Vasile_Chitaru "Vasile Chitaru"), and Ilie.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Sport Club played well in the [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României"), where, after the [Romanian Revolution](/wiki/Romanian_Revolution "Romanian Revolution"), they reached the final at the end of the [1990–91](/wiki/1990%E2%80%9391_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1990–91 Cupa României") season before losing 2–1 to [Universitatea Craiova](/wiki/CS_Universitatea_Craiova "CS Universitatea Craiova"). Universitatea Craiova was also the winner of [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") that season, which allowed the yellow-and-blues to qualify for the second and final time in a European competition (the UEFA Cup). FC Bacău was drawn to meet [Werder Bremen](/wiki/SV_Werder_Bremen "SV Werder Bremen"), which would defeat [AS Monaco](/wiki/AS_Monaco_FC "AS Monaco FC") 2–0 in the [Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "UEFA Cup Winners' Cup"). Werder Bremen was a tall, strong, cosmopolitan squad; FC Bacău was a little-known team with serious financial problems.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Sechelariu era (1992–2014)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sechelariu era (1992–2014)")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Years |
| Dinamo Bacău | 1950–1970 |
| SC Bacău | 1970–1990 |
| FC Bacău | 1990–1992 |
| Selena Bacău | 1992–1995 |
| AS Bacău | 1995–1997 |
| FCM Bacău | 1997–2014 |
| Inactive | 2014–2019 |
| FCM Bacău | 2019–2020 |

From 1992 to 2006, the team remained in [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") as Selena, AS and FCM; Dumitru Sechelariu was its owner.[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1) It was a period of relative growth; the team finished 11th in [1995–96](/wiki/1995%E2%80%9396_Divizia_A "1995–96 Divizia A") (when they won 5–1 at [Steaua Stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Steaua_(1974) "Stadionul Steaua (1974)")), fifth in [1996–97](/wiki/1996%E2%80%9397_Divizia_A "1996–97 Divizia A"), 10th in [1997–98](/wiki/1997%E2%80%9398_Divizia_A "1997–98 Divizia A"), fifth in [1998–99](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_Divizia_A "1998–99 Divizia A"), and eighth in [1999–2000](/wiki/1999%E2%80%932000_Divizia_A "1999–2000 Divizia A"). They were relegated in the summer of 2001 after a relegation play-off against [Farul Constanța](/wiki/FC_Farul_Constan%C8%9Ba "FC Farul Constanța").[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1) The [2001–02](/wiki/2001%E2%80%9302_Divizia_A "2001–02 Divizia A") season began with high hopes, great investments and a 4–2 win against [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București") before a sixth place. After 2002, FCM Bacău was in the lower part of the tables and was relegated to [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II") at the end of the [2005–06](/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_Divizia_A "2005–06 Divizia A") season. Sechelariu referred to FCM Bacău as "my child", "dear", but also "disabled".[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1)

At the end of the [2006–07](/wiki/2006%E2%80%9307_Liga_II "2006–07 Liga II") season, the team finished in fifth place; in the [following season](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Liga_II "2007–08 Liga II"), they were one step away from relegation to the third tier. For the [2009–10](/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_Liga_II "2009–10 Liga II") season, the team focused on promotion to [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"); due to financial problems and misunderstandings between Dumitru Sechelariu and the municipal council, FCM was relegated to [Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") after [forfeiting](/wiki/Forfeit_(sport) "Forfeit (sport)") two matches.[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1)

The municipality of Bacău registered the team for the following season in [Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") as SCM Bacău.[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1) On 9 June 2010, it was announced that a new club would be developed by the municipality; Sechelariu became a minority shareholder. The new management of the yellow-and-blues decided to change their name to SC FCM Bacău SA and terminate the contracts of former AS FCM Bacău players. FCM Bacău was promoted to Liga II in 2011, finishing seventh the following season. Before the next season, the players left the club for financial reasons (most for [FC Botoșani](/wiki/FC_Boto%C8%99ani "FC Botoșani")) and Sechelariu transferred the club entirely to the municipality. With the team facing relegation to the third league, the municipality decided to stop investing in the club and re-direct public financing to CS Mesagerul Bacău (which would be renamed [SC Bacău](/wiki/ACS_Gauss_Bac%C4%83u "ACS Gauss Bacău")). After Sechelariu's declining health and death in early 2013, the club withdrew from the league at the start of the season and was dissolved.[[1]](#cite_note-urban-1)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

### Supporters, the last hope (2017–2020)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Supporters, the last hope (2017–2020)")]

The Supporters Association of FCM Bacău, supported by Sergiu Sechelariu (Dumitru's brother and owner of the FCM 1950 Bacău brand), began a collaboration with Gauss Bacău (the former SC Bacău) in 2017. The collaboration broke down during the summer of 2018; after a year of inactivity, fans (supported again by Sergiu Sechelariu) began the ASS FCM 1950 Bacău. They enrolled the senior squad in Liga IV, but the team was again dissolved at the end of the 2019–20 season.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Grounds

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Grounds")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/31/BacauStadium.jpg/250px-BacauStadium.jpg)](/wiki/File:BacauStadium.jpg)

Stadionul Municipal

Main article: [Stadionul Municipal (Bacău)](/wiki/Stadionul_Municipal_(Bac%C4%83u) "Stadionul Municipal (Bacău)")

Until 1966 FCM Bacău (named *Dinamo Bacău*) used to play its home matches on Steaua Roșie Stadium, with a capacity of 5,000 people, but moved subsequently on the [Municipal Stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Municipal_(Bac%C4%83u) "Stadionul Municipal (Bacău)") in [Bacău](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u "Bacău"), with a capacity of 17,500 seats. Seen as a coquettish stadium of the country, especially during Sechelariu's era, when the owner of the team Dumitru Sechelariu renovated and modernized it and assured the installation of a floodlight system. Between 2001 and 2004, the stadium was named as *Dumitru Sechelariu*, after the name of the eccentric businessman, mayor of Bacău and owner of FCM. The death of Sechelariu meant the death of football from [Bacău](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u "Bacău"), after FCM, the stadium was also abandoned, being the victim of an unsuccessful modernization project. The stadium which is situated in the center of the city and was only some time ago the home of FCM is now a ruin.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

After the re-foundation as **ASS FCM Bacău**, the club has to play its home matches on Baza Sportivă Nautică Șerbănești, with a capacity of only 200 people.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Support

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Support")]

FCM Bacău has many supporters in [Bacău](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u "Bacău") and especially in [Bacău County](/wiki/Bac%C4%83u_County "Bacău County"). The ultras groups of FCM Bacău are known as *Taurii furioși*, *Best* and *Ultra Boys*. The three groups merged in 2010 and form *FCM 1950 Bacău*, group that developed subsequently in an association that managed to bring back to life the club from the city of [George Bacovia](/wiki/George_Bacovia "George Bacovia").[[20]](#cite_note-ultras-20) In the past were also another ultras groups such as *RSB*, *Prima-Linie* or *FRT Bacău*.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

FCM Bacău fans have an intense rivalry with the supporters of [Ceahlăul Piatra Neamț](/wiki/CSM_Ceahl%C4%83ul_Piatra_Neam%C8%9B "CSM Ceahlăul Piatra Neamț"), which are known under the name of *Urșii carpatini*.[[22]](#cite_note-clasico-22)

### Rivalries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FCM_Bac%C4%83u&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Rivalries")]

The most important rivalry for FCM Bacău is the one against [Ceahlăul Piatra Neamț](/wiki/CSM_Ceahl%C4%83ul_Piatra_Neam%C8%9B "CSM Ceahlăul Piatra Neamț"). This match is known as the *Derby of Moldavia* or *Il Classico*.[[22]](#cite_note-clasico-22) Over time, FCM had important rivalries with other teams, such as [Steaua București](/wiki/FCSB "FCSB"), [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București") or [Rapid București](/wiki/FC_Rapid_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Rapid București"), as well as some regional ones and even local, for example against [SC Bacău](/wiki/ACS_Gauss_Bac%C4%83u "ACS Gauss Bacău").[[20]](#cite_note-ultras-20)
